howItWorks.intro: This audio file size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: WAV Size = Sample Rate × (Bit Depth ÷ 8) × Channels × Duration
Uncompressed Bitrate = Sample Rate × Bit Depth × Channels
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How big is a 3-minute WAV file?
faq.answer: A 3-minute stereo 44.1kHz/16-bit WAV file is about 30 MB. At 48kHz/24-bit (studio quality), it's about 50 MB. Higher sample rates and bit depths increase size proportionally.

faq.question: How much smaller is FLAC than WAV?
faq.answer: FLAC typically compresses to 50-60% of the WAV size with zero quality loss. A 30 MB WAV becomes about 16-18 MB FLAC. Compression varies with audio content.

faq.question: What sample rate and bit depth should I record at?
faq.answer: For most recording: 48kHz/24-bit is the professional standard. 44.1kHz/16-bit is CD quality. 96kHz is useful for recordings that will be heavily processed or pitch-shifted.

howWeCalculate.intro: This audio file size calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.
